scope_note: QA Pell Sturmian Bridge cert [295]. Primary sources: Lothaire, M. (2002) Algebraic Combinatorics on Words Cambridge University Press ISBN 978-0-521-81220-7 Ch.2 (Sturmian words, complexity p(n)=n+1, characteristic word s_alpha, CF connection); Hardy, G.H. and Wright, E.M. (2008) An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers Oxford ISBN 978-0-19-921986-5 Ch.X (continued fractions, convergents, best approximations). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): The characteristic Sturmian word s_alpha for alpha=sqrt(2)-1=[0;2,2,2,...] satisfies: (1) STURMIAN_COMPLEXITY: p(n)=n+1 for all n>=1 (complexity function = minimal for aperiodic binary word); (2) STURMIAN_APERIODIC: s_alpha has no period T<=50; (3) GAP23: consecutive 1-positions in s_alpha have gaps in {2,3} = {floor(1/alpha), ceil(1/alpha)} = {floor(sqrt(2)+1), ceil(sqrt(2)+1)}; (4) PELL_E_CONVERGENTS: the denominators of CF convergents to alpha=[0;2,2,...] are exactly the Pell e-values {1,2,5,12,29,...} satisfying q_{n+1}=2q_n+q_{n-1}; (5) RLLR_PERIODIC: the word (RLLR)^k from cert [294] has period 4 and complexity [2,4,4,4,...] -- NOT Sturmian. BRIDGE: Pell e_n=q_n because both sequences satisfy the SAME recurrence q_{n+1}=2q_n+q_{n-1} with the SAME initial conditions (q_0=1,q_1=2), arising from the period-2 CF structures sqrt(2)=[1;2-bar] and sqrt(2)-1=[0;2-bar]. The SB-path word (RLLR)_inf encodes sqrt(2)>1 (periodic); s_alpha encodes sqrt(2)-1<1 (Sturmian). Theorem NT: Sturmian word computed via integer floor arithmetic; Pell recurrence over integers. Mechanism chain: cert [294] (SL(2,Z) spine, (RLLR)_inf); cert [289] (Koenig Pell Ford, Pell e-values). Lineage: derived 2026-06-01.
